sql_array: CREATE TABLE xml_documents (id INT PRIMARY KEY, doc_content LONG VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO xml_documents (id, doc_content) VALUES (2, xper_doc('<root><child attr="1">Sample</child></root>'));
SELECT id, xper_doc('<root><child>Data</child><child attr="2">More Data</child></root>') AS tree FROM xml_documents WHERE id >= 1;
SELECT id, tree FROM (SELECT id, xper_doc(doc_content) AS tree FROM xml_documents) AS trees WHERE id >= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM xml_documents) - 1;
SELECT id, xper_doc('<root><child>Test</child></root>') AS tree FROM xml_documents WHERE xper_doc(doc_content) >= xper_doc('<root><child>Empty</child></root>');
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM xml_documents GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) >= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM xml_documents WHERE doc_content IS NOT NULL);
WITH RECURSIVE xml_trees AS (SELECT id, xper_doc(doc_content) AS tree FROM xml_documents) SELECT * FROM xml_trees WHERE id >= 1;
CREATE TABLE xml_test (id INT, value VARCHAR(255), content LONG VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO xml_test (id, value, content) VALUES (1, 'test', xper_doc('<data/>')), (2, 'example', xper_doc('<data><child>1</child></data>'));
SELECT id, value FROM xml_test WHERE xper_doc(content) >= xper_doc('<data><child>0</child></data>') AND value IS NOT NULL;
